title: Chettinad Varutharacha Mutton Curry

subs.name: 700 g chicken with bone — pressure-cook 3 whistles only
4 tbsp desiccated coconut, dry-roasted in the same step
reduce to 3 pieces and add 1 tsp Kashmiri chilli powder for colour without extra heat
coconut oil, which complements the roasted coconut paste

steps.text: In a dry kadai over low heat, combine dried red chillies, coriander seeds, black peppercorns, fennel, cumin, star anise, cinnamon, cloves and cardamom. Toast, stirring continuously, for 3 minutes until the spices darken by one shade and release a deep, complex aroma.

Add the grated coconut to the kadai and continue to toast on low, stirring, for a further 2 minutes until the coconut turns golden-brown — not dark. Transfer the entire mixture to a mixie and grind to a smooth, thick paste with 6 tablespoons of water. Set aside.
Heat gingelly oil in a pressure cooker over medium heat. Add curry leaves, then shallots, and fry until golden, about 5 minutes. Add ginger-garlic paste and cook 2 minutes.

Add tomatoes and cook until they soften and the oil separates. Add turmeric and the mutton pieces. Stir well to coat the meat, then add 200 ml water and salt. Seal the cooker and pressure-cook on high until 5 whistles, then lower to medium and cook 10 minutes. Release pressure naturally.
Open the pressure cooker and check that the mutton is tender. Transfer to a kadai if your pressure cooker base is narrow. Stir in the roasted coconut-spice paste and mix until fully incorporated.

Simmer the curry uncovered on medium heat for 12 minutes, stirring every few minutes, until the gravy thickens, darkens and oil floats to the surface. The paste will cook into the gravy and the raw masala smell will give way to a roasted, nutty fragrance.
Taste and correct salt. Rest the curry for 10 minutes off the heat before serving — the flavour deepens considerably as it rests. Serve with steamed rice, idiyappam or parotta.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
safety_warnings: Keep raw animal products, their plates and utensils separate from cooked food; wash hands and surfaces after contact.
Follow the cooker manufacturer's fill, venting and opening instructions; never force the lid open under pressure.
critical_checks: Use an appropriate food thermometer and safe doneness guidance for the specific cut of meat.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: mutton → 700 g chicken with bone — pressure-cook 3 whistles only; coconut → 4 tbsp desiccated coconut, dry-roasted in the same step; gingelly-oil → coconut oil, which complements the roasted coconut paste; dried-red-chilli → reduce to 3 pieces and add 1 tsp Kashmiri chilli powder for colour without extra heat
